Fire protection sprinkler system designed to satisfy requirements outlined in NFPA13D Standard for Installation of Systems in One and Two Family Dwellings and Manufactured Homes.The unique flag-shaped layout of the property leads to a specific non-compliance issue with the Ontario Building Code regarding fire access routes(OBC 3.2.5.5.). The access route to the proposed garden suite surpasses the maximum allowed length as defined in the code, particularly concerning the distances between the building's principal entrance, required access openings, and the nearest portion of the fire department's access route.Given the unique flag-shaped layout of the property, which results in a specific non-compliance issue with the Ontario Building Code regarding fire access routes, we are proposing the installation of a Fire Sprinkler System in accordance with the NFPA 130 Code as an alternative solution. This measure is aimed at ensuring the safety of residents by compensating for the identified compliance shortfall.

Gerrard Station is an elevated, non-terminal station on the Ontario line. It is located at the intersection of Gerrard Street East and Carlaw Avenue. The station'slocation features entrances off both sides of the intersection, as well as connections on street to the existing streetcar and bus network. Riverdale ShoppingCentre is also located nearby. The station¿s main entrance is located on the corner of Gerrard Street East and Carlaw Avenue, and is designated as thesouthwest entrance and faces the secondary entrance. The secondary entrance is located on the opposite corner of the intersection, designated as northwestentrance.

We are proposing to renovate a partial area of the 2nd floor Conference Center for the University of Toronto. The extent of the renovation includes building a Universal washroom, providing new finishes to the walls, ceilings and floors. We are also proposing to retrofit the existing Air Handling Units which provides both heating, cooling and exhausting for the 2nd floor and 3rd floor washrooms. One existing roof exhaust opening is to be capped and a new exhaust fan is designed in the 2nd floor ceiling. Additional plumbing alterations are also proposed for the re-routing of plumbing lines and upgrading in piping materials. Furthermore, the renovation also includes new electrical outlets, fixtures, data, and AV equipment to serve the new renovated Conference Center spaces on the second floor. To complete this renovation, mechanical and electrical work is expected to take place on the ground, 2nd, 3rd and 4th floors. The majority of the Architectural work will be on the 2nd floor, with patching work on the ground and 3rd floors.

Site plan application to permit the proposal for a 14-storey mixed use building with 19,483 square metres of GFA, including 462 squrae metres of commercial space and 360 square metres of daycare space, resulting in an FSI of 4.0. The building would step down to 6 storeys on Cocksfield Avenue. There would be 270 (rental) residential units, including 54 (20%) affordable (rental) units. There would be two levels of underground parking containing a total of 213 parking spaces, including 197 parking spaces for residents (including 9 barrier-free parking spaces) and 16 parking spaces for commercial uses and visitors.   [Zoning By Law Amendment Application: 20 205918 NNY 06 OZ /Rental Housing Application: 20 205924 NNY 06 RH]

Site Plan Application for a stepped 12-storey multi-unit residential building, inclusive of a rooftop mechanical/amenity floor, and residential uses at-grade at the rear and above. Approximately 18,350.1 sq m (GFA), with a density of 5.0 FSI. 287 apartment dwellings consisting of 1 bachelor unit (0.3%), 98 one-bedroom units (34.1%), 94 one-bedroom plus den units (32.8%), 34 two-bedroom units (11.8%), 28 two-bedroom plus den units (9.8%), and 32 three-bedroom units (11.1%). There will be two levels of underground parking consisting of 158 vehicle parking spaces, and an additional 2 car share parking spaces and 2 visitor spaces at grade for a total of 162 parking spaces at the site

Zoning By Law Amendment Application for 1-3 Concorde Gate and 10-12 Concorde Place. The Rezoning Application and associated Draft Plan of Subdivision application proposes to demolish the existing office buildings on the lands at 1-3 Concorde Gate and 10-12 Concorde Place.  Application proposes to redevelop the lands with five residential and mixed-use buildings comprised of nine towers ranging from 40 to 52 storeys. The proposed development  also includes two new public parks located at the north and south ends of the site, which comprise a total of 3,690 square metres in size, in addition to a new public street which will bisect the 10-12 Concorde Place property in a general north-south direction. Overall the application proposes 4,086 dwelling units and 307,004 square metres of residential space, 841 square metres of retail and 437 square metres of community space at grade. The overall gross floor area proposed is 308,284 square metres, resulting in a gross density of 9.95 FSI.  See 21 179556 NNY 16 SB  for the associated Draft Plan of Subdivision file.  See OLT website for OLT appeal information (File No. OLT-22-002185). The application has been approved by the OLT.

INACTIVE The subject property contains two 8 storey residential apartment buildings that are linked by a below grade garage.  The proposal is to construct an addition to the southern building (515 Chaplin) by adding 69 new units to the southeast end in an area of the site that contains a surface parking lot.  There would be 18 existing rental units demolished and replaced by 18 new rental units plus an additional 69 rental units.  The current building contains 221 units and the total unit count after construction would be 290 units.  The proposed building height is 13 storeys with the roof being used for a mechanical penthouse and outdoor amenity area.

Proposed redevelopment of the 'Newtonbrook Plaza' and adjacent lands for a mixed-use project with 1,674 residential units in 3 blocks with 4 buildings of 28, 36, 36 and 44 storeys, at-grade retail uses, office uses, townhouses and a new public park and street.

Rezoning application to complement appeal to Official Plan Amendment 388 for the subject lands in order to permit its re-development for the pruposes of a 26 storey new mixed use building complete with 340 dwelling units above retail and office type uses..  Included the propsal is the request to assign a hold to a portion of the lands in order to address the flood plain management within the Port Lands.

Applications for Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment/Site Plan Control to permit the development of eighteen (18) townhouse dwelling units with a total residential gross floor area of 3,769 m² resulting in a density of 1.44 FSI. 36 tandem parking spaces are proposed for residents and visitors in private integral garages accessed via a private mews from Churchill Avenue. Concurrent Official Plan/Zoning By-law Amendment Application: 14 260003 NNY 23 OZ

Revised:  A four storey residential building containing 26 residential units (7 - one-bedroom units and 19 - two-bedroom units) and one level of underground parking accessed directly off of Church Avenue.  All these units will be purpose built rental housing.

The application proposes to redevelop the subject lands (Block B) with two rental apartment buildings of 38-storeys (Building B1) and 44-storeys (Building B2), connected by a 4-storey podium. The proposed development would have a total gross floor area of 69,002 square metres consisting of 68,277 square metres of residential uses and 725 square metres of retail/commercial space. The application proposes a total of 778 residential units. A total of 533 vehicular and 679 bicycle parking spaces would be provided to service the proposed development. A landscape courtyard and promenade has been incorporated in the proposal to connect the proposed development to the existing buildings at 100, 200 and 300 Consilium Place.

Site Plan Approval application to facilitate the development of Block 4 of the former LCBO lands, part of the comprehensively planned Lower Yonge Precinct.  Blocks 1 and 2 of the LCBO lands are already under development, and Block 3 has been conveyed as a City Park. The proposal is comprised of 3 towers having heights of 70, 78, and 85 storeys atop individual mixed-use podiums.  The north building includes a 990 m² daycare and space for a 5,613 m² elementary public school, both to be located in the existing heritage building on site.  A total of 1,291 m² of non-residential floor area is proposed as well as 173,713 m² of residential GFA, resulting in 2,606 residential units including 178 affordable housing units.

RENTAL HOUSING APPLICATION: 20 205924 NNY 06 RH  This application proposes to amend the Sheppard West / Dublin Secondary Plan, former City of North York Zoning By-law 7625 and City of Toronto Zoning By-law 569-2013 for the property at 824 Sheppard Avenue West, 177, 179 and 181 Cocksfield Avenue to permit a 14-storey mixed use building with 19,483 square metres of GFA, including 462 squrae metres of commercial space and 360 square metres of daycare space, resulting in an FSI of 4.0. The building would step down to 6 storeys on Cocksfield Avenue. There would be 270 (rental) residential units, including 54 (20%) affordable (rental) units. There would be two levels of underground parking containing a total of 213 parking spaces, including 197 parking spaces for residents (including 9 barrier-free parking spaces) and 16 parking spaces for commercial uses and visitors.

A Rezoning Amendment and Draft Plan of Subdivision application to re-develop the site with 6 new residential buildings,  a community centre, a public park, public roads and private driveways.  The buildings would range in height from 4 storeys to 25 storeys and contain a total of 941 new dwelling units consisting of townhouses and apartment units of which 341 are TCHC units and 600 are market units.  The units would consist of townhouses and apartment units.  The existing 152 TCHC apartment units at 5 Needle Firway would be retained.  Building One would contain 123 TCHC units, Building Two would contain 218 TCHC units, Building Three would contain a community centre and daycare facilities, Building Four would contain 283 market units, Building Five would contain 48 market units, Building 7a would contain 142 market units and Building 7b would contain 127 market units.   A total of 667 parking spaces would be provided site wide.Properties include:  3, 36 Marsh Grassway, 7, 11 Blue Grassway, 4, 17 Cane Grassway, 5, 10, 40 Turf Grassway and 1,2, & 8 Dune Grassway.

Construction of a 3-storey building for water treatment process upgrades, including ultraviolet (UV) disinfection and a residuals management facility (RMF). This building, which will typically be unoccupied, has an approximate building area of 2,505 m2 (approximately 71.91m x 35.48m) and a gross floor area of 5,221 m2. There are varying rooftop heights ranging from approximately 8 m to 24 m above grade. There will be 3 new parking spaces available in the drive area. There will also be another single-storey building constructed to house electrical equipment, with an approximate area of 46 m2 (dimensions of 11 m x 4.7 m), along with additional infrastructure work throughout the site. The height of the building is approximately 3.9 m above grade. This building will typically be unoccupied.The project also includes renewable energy infrastructure (solar photovoltaic panels and battery energy storage system, i.e. solar panels) to be installed south of the proposed building area as part of another phase of construction work.

Subdivision application for 1-3 Concorde Gate and 10-12 Concorde Place. The Draft Plan of Subdivision application and associated Rezoning Application proposes to demolish the existing office buildings on the lands at 1-3 Concorde Gate and 10-12 Concorde Place.  Application proposes to redevelop the lands with five residential and mixed-use buildings comprised of nine towers ranging from 40 to 52 storeys. The proposed development  also includes two new public parks located at the north and south ends of the site, which comprise a total of 3,690 square metres in size, in addition to a new public street which will bisect the 10-12 Concorde Place property in a general north-south direction. Overall the application proposes 4,086 dwelling units and 307,004 square metres of residential space, 841 square metres of retail and 437 square metres of community space at grade. The Draft Plan of Subdivision application and associated Rezoning Application proposes to demolish the existing office buildings on the lands at 1-3 Concorde Gate and 10-12 Concorde Place.  Application proposes to redevelop the lands with five residential and mixed-use buildings comprised of nine towers ranging from 40 to 52 storeys. The proposed development  also includes two new public parks located at the north and south ends of the site, which comprise a total of 3,690 square metres in size, in addition to a new public street which will bisect the 10-12 Concorde Place property in a general north-south direction. Overall the application proposes 4,086 dwelling units and 307,004 square metres of residential space, 841 square metres of retail and 437 square metres of community space at grade. The overall gross floor area proposed is 308,284 square metres, resulting in a gross density of 9.95 FSI.  See 21179555 16 OZ  for the associated Rezoning Application file
